g.title: steward_journey
g.text: VCL — Steward Journey (drift → alert → SEE → REVIEW → deliver)

g.title: src
g.text: a rule/schema changes, or a scan fails

g.title: enf1
g.text: re-read live state, compare vs sealed anchors

g.title: adv
g.text: SEPARATE advisory step — the AI lives here, not in enforce
+ classification — DISPLAY ONLY, the gate never reads it

g.title: block
g.text: Agent → wrapper lookup_context → wrapper reads the verdict → unverified
→ WITHHOLD + honest note ("do not infer or fabricate")
the action tool ALSO refuses on the verdict — two structural layers

g.title: alert
g.text: → Slack + email: SEE url + REVIEW deep link

g.title: see
g.text: reads which dimension drifted + the AI cosmetic/substantive assessment

g.title: actsub
g.text: 2. REVIEW — the deep link opens the SUBSTANTIVE walkthrough
EVERY alert lands here first. This file has NO re-certify command.
The re-certify command is reachable ONLY via the onward link below,
after the steward reads the classification — structural, not a warning.

g.title: onward
g.text: onward LINK (a link, not a command)

g.title: s1
g.text: substantive: investigate — which rule changed, confirm with the owner
NO re-certify command exists on this path

g.title: c3
g.text: — DISPLAY ONLY, a SEPARATE chained step (not seal)

g.title: deliver
g.text: Agent retry → wrapper reads verdict → verified

g.title: s2
g.text: until the change is resolved (the safe default)

g.title: footer
g.text: The human AUTHORIZES; the deterministic engine CERTIFIES — no status field is ever
hand-flipped. The VERDICT (verification aspect) and the DISPLAY (governance-drift aspect)
are written by DIFFERENT components. Every alert defaults to the no-seal file; the seal is
reachable only after passing through the file that requires reading the classification.
